cards.text:
The Curiosity of Human Beings
Humans are naturally curious and enjoy exploring their surroundings from childhood, sparking interest in science.

Introduction to Science
Science is a way of understanding the world through thinking, observing, and doing.

The Universality of Science
Science is present everywhere, from the ocean's depths to outer space's vastness.

Unraveling Mysteries
Science helps unravel the mysteries of nature, such as why stars shine or how flowers bloom.

The Science Puzzle
Science resembles a never-ending jigsaw puzzle, with each discovery adding another piece.

Exploration of Earth
Earth is unique in supporting life, and its environment requires protection and understanding.

Food and Science
Exploring how plants and animals grow involves understanding food, its importance, and its diversity.

The Fascination with Water
Water is essential for survival and is intriguing in its states, from liquid to solid to gas.

Material World
The world consists of various materials; science helps identify and separate these.

Out-Worldly Inquiries
Curiosity extends beyond Earth to the Sun, the Moon, and the countless stars in the sky.

The Scientific Method
  • Observe something interesting.
  • Think of a question about it.
  • Guess a possible answer.
  • Test through experiments or observations.
  • Analyze the results.

The Scientist in Everyone
Engaging with the scientific process in daily life situations makes us all scientists.

Collaboration in Science
Scientists work together globally, and discoveries are more fun when shared with friends.

Lifelong Science Journey
Science is a continuous journey beyond school, sparking inquiry and problem-solving.

Joyful Scientific Exploration
Science embodies joyful exploration and discovery, urging perpetual curiosity and exploration.
